title: pilosella floribunda subsp. atrocrocea
intro: Pilosella floribunda subsp. atrocrocea, also known as False Sowthistle or Yellow Sow Thistle, is a plant species within the dandelion family (Asteraceae), characterized by its bright yellow flowers and deeply divided leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and digestive-aiding compounds. The health benefits of Pilosella floribunda subsp. atrocrocea include its ability to alleviate digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system. The plant can be prepared as an infusion, tincture, or poultice, allowing for a variety of herbal preparations to be used in traditional medicine.
